@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent incorrectly filtered support cases by matching Case.orderitemid__c to the product Id (e.g., call_W69ujVC6k5RL3IZv8xKBsadd returned []), and after an incomplete manual IN list check (call_E3tcG3s2TJhzer5p1ugubDo7 also []), it concluded “insufficient data” instead of properly linking Case.orderitemid__c to OrderItem.Product2Id and identifying the November spike.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: yes
FM4: no
@@

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent filtered support.Case rows by comparing orderitemid__c/issueid__c directly to the Product2 Id (01tWt000006hVJdIAM), leading to empty results across queries (e.g., var_call_wu82C3Qfmr6rR8RlwACglrWZ) and concluding "No cases found" instead of joining Case.orderitemid__c to OrderItem and filtering by Product2Id to identify November.

FM3: yes
FM4: no

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After earlier empty-result attempts, the agent’s final contributing query aggregated all cases from 2020-06-10 to 2021-04-10 without filtering by the specified product and then returned None, stopping before identifying a month with significantly higher counts, so the plan could not produce the required month name.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no

The agent filtered support.Case records directly by orderitemid__c equals the Product2 Id (calls returning [] and 0), instead of mapping Case→OrderItem→Product2 to filter by the given product, so the monthly counts query yielded no rows and it returned "No data" instead of identifying November.
B. Failure modes encountered:
